user_request: Draft a Summary Suit under Order XXXVII CPC for recovery of Rs.9,00,000/- based on a dishonoured cheque. Cheque No. [CHEQUE_NUMBER] dated [CHEQUE_DATE] drawn on [DRAWEE_BANK], [BRANCH] Branch, Bengaluru. Cheque was returned unpaid with bank return memo endorsed 'Funds Insufficient' dated [RETURN_DATE]. Statutory legal notice was issued and duly served on the Defendant. Defendant failed to pay despite notice. Claim principal Rs.9,00,000/-, interest at 18% per annum from cheque date till realization, and costs. Draft in Commercial Court format suitable for filing before the Commercial Court at Bengaluru. Include Order XXXVII maintainability paragraph, cause of action, limitation, valuation, court fee and prayer clause. Plead limitation properly.

facts.summary: Summary Suit under Order XXXVII CPC for recovery of Rs.9,00,000 based on a dishonoured cheque issued by the Defendant. The cheque was returned unpaid due to insufficient funds, and a statutory legal notice was served, but the Defendant failed to make payment.

stage of determination of the matter in issue will arise in a summary suit only after the defendant obtains leave.

An Act to consolidate and amend the law for the limitation of suits and other proceedings and for 
purposes connected therewith.

the case may be;

harmoniously so that the objects of both are not frustrated. The words trial of any suit will have to be construed 
in the context of provisions of O 37 of the Code. The scheme of summary procedure provided by O 37 is that a 
stage of determination of the matter in issue will arise in a summary suit only after the defendant obtains leave.

ACT NO. 36 OF 1963 
[5th October, 1963.] 
An Act to consolidate and amend the law for the limitation of suits and other proceedings and for 
purposes connected therewith. 
BE it enacted by Parliament in the Fourteenth Year of the Republic of India as follows:— 
PART I 
PRELIMINARY 
1. Short title, extent and commencement.—(1) This Act may be called the Limitation Act, 1963.

Official Gazette, appoint.

the case may be;

(i) any person from or through whom a defendant derives his liability to be sued; 
(ii) any person whose estate is represented by the defendant as executor, administrator

Thus, in a suit for permanent injunction and restraining the defendant from putting up construction in the house 
agreed upon to be sold, the plaintiff amended the plaint to include additional relief of specific performance of sale 
agreement, but the requisite court-fee for additional relief was not paid. The order rejecting the plaint partly in 
respect of the additional relief was not challenged. Subsequent payment of court-fee is not valid payment. 21 In 
another case of injunction for restraining the electricity board from disconnecting the electric supply, the suit was

valorem court-fee was legal. The High Courts observed that mere astuteness in drafting the plaint would not glaze 
the jurisdiction of court for looking at the substance of the relief claimed for.22

draft_artifacts.title: Summary Suit under Order XXXVII CPC for Recovery of Money based on Dishonoured Cheque
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COMMERCIAL COURT AT BENGALURU
CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}
(Under Order XXXVII of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

IN THE MATTER OF:
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
{{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
                                    ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
{{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
                                    ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF RS. 9,00,000/- WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
UNDER ORDER XXXVII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

TO
THE HON'BLE THE JUDGE,
COMMERCIAL COURT AT BENGALURU

The Plaintiff above-named respectfully submits as follows:

1. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try this suit as the cause of action arose wholly within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, where the cheque in question was presented for payment and was dishonoured. Courts within whose jurisdiction cheques were dishonoured will have jurisdiction to entertain the suit.

2. That this suit is filed under Order XXXVII of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, read with Rule 1(2)(a) thereof, as it is a suit upon a bill of exchange (cheque) being a suit for recovery of a debt or liquidated demand in money payable by the Defendant. The Plaintiff specifically avers that this suit is filed under Order XXXVII CPC and that no relief which does not fall within the ambit of this Order has been claimed in the plaint.

3. That the Plaintiff is {{PLAINTIFF_DESCRIPTION}} and is carrying on business/residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

4. That the Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_DESCRIPTION}} and resides/carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

5. That in the course of business transactions between the parties, the Defendant issued Cheque No. {{CHEQUE_NUMBER}} dated {{CHEQUE_DATE}} drawn on {{DRAWEE_BANK}}, {{BRANCH}} Branch, Bengaluru, for a sum of Rs. 9,00,000/- (Rupees Nine Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff towards discharge of a legally enforceable debt/liability.

6. That the said cheque was presented for payment by the Plaintiff on {{PRESENTMENT_DATE}} through the Plaintiff's bankers.

7. That the said cheque was returned unpaid by the drawee bank on {{RETURN_DATE}} with the endorsement "Funds Insufficient" as evidenced by the return memo dated {{RETURN_DATE}}.

8. That thereafter, the Plaintiff issued a statutory legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} demanding payment of the said amount of Rs. 9,00,000/- within the statutory period from the date of receipt thereof, calling upon the Defendant to pay the said amount.

9. That the said notice was duly served upon the Defendant on {{NOTICE_SERVICE_DATE}}.

10. That despite the service of the said notice and the lapse of statutory period, the Defendant has failed and neglected to pay the said amount or any part thereof till date.

11. CAUSE OF ACTION:
    (a) That the underlying obligation/right arose on {{CHEQUE_DATE}} when the Defendant issued the cheque in discharge of the debt;
    (b) That the right to sue accrued to the Plaintiff on {{RETURN_DATE}} when the cheque was dishonoured and returned unpaid by the bank;
    (c) That the cause of action is not of a continuing nature but arose on the date of dishonour, though the debt continues to be owed by the Defendant.

12. LIMITATION:
    That the present suit is filed within the prescribed period of limitation of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action, i.e., from {{RETURN_DATE}}. The suit is filed well within the period prescribed under {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Limitation Act, 1963, and is not barred by limitation.

13. VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
    That for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 9,00,000/- (Rupees Nine Lakhs only). The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

14. That the Defendant is liable to pay the principal amount of Rs. 9,00,000/- along with interest thereon at the rate of 18% per annum from {{CHEQUE_DATE}} till the date of realization of the decretal amount, besides costs of the suit.

PRAYER:
    It is therefore most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 9,00,000/- (Rupees Nine Lakhs only);
    (b) Award interest on the said principal sum at the rate of 18% per annum from {{CHEQUE_DATE}} till the date of realization;
    (c) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (d) Pass such other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

    AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF AS IN DUTY BOUND SHALL EVER PRAY.

VERIFICATION:
    Verified at Bengaluru on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}} that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 4, 8, 9 and 13 are true to my knowledge and the contents of paragraphs 5 to 7, 10 to 12 and 14 are based on legal advice and records maintained in the ordinary course of business, believed to be true.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS
1. Cheque No. {{CHEQUE_NUMBER}} dated {{CHEQUE_DATE}} - Annexure P-1
2. Bank Return Memo dated {{RETURN_DATE}} - Annexure P-2
3. Legal Notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} - Annexure P-3
4. Proof of Service of Notice - Annexure P-4

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE}}

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate for Plaintiff
